name: CUID2 (Collision-Resistant IDs)

performance.notes: Cryptographically secure; ~0.01ms per ID on modern hardware

agent_readiness.notes: Next-generation collision-resistant IDs. Replaces CUID v1. createId() generates secure 24-char IDs starting with 'c'. Advantages over UUID v4: URL-safe, sortable prefix (timestamp component), no dashes, shorter. Advantages over nanoid: guaranteed collision resistance proofs, consistent length. Uses Web Crypto API (Node 14+/browser). Custom length via init({length}). ESM + CJS. Used in Prisma as default ID strategy. Better than UUID v4 for database primary keys (sortable, friendly). ULID is alternative if strict time-ordering needed.

gotchas: Requires Node 14+ for Web Crypto API
Not strictly time-sortable like ULID (but has timestamp prefix)
ESM default in newer versions — CJS via require works
best_when: Database primary keys needing URL-safe, readable IDs
Distributed systems needing collision-resistant IDs without coordination
When UUID v4 is needed but shorter/URL-safe format preferred
avoid_when: When strict lexicographic time-ordering required (use ULID)
When UUID standard compatibility required (use uuid package)
When monotonic sequence within a millisecond matters
summary: CUID2 generates collision-resistant, URL-safe IDs — the modern replacement for CUID v1 and a better database key alternative to UUID v4. createId() produces secure 24-char IDs. URL-safe, no dashes, timestamp prefix for rough ordering. Uses Web Crypto API. Default ID strategy in Prisma. Best for database primary keys where short, URL-safe, collision-resistant IDs are preferred over standard UUIDs.
